vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1901: .gitattributes declares 'merge=beads' but merge.beads.driver is never configured — .beads/.pan state still conflict-storms (PAN-1841 fix inert)

plan.title: Make .beads/.pan tracked state merge conflict-free (fix the inert PAN-1841 driver)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: PAN-1841 added `.beads/issues.jsonl merge=beads` to .gitattributes but never configured `merge.beads.driver`, so git silently ignores the attribute and falls back to the default conflict merge. `bd` has no merge-driver subcommand (`bd merge` is unknown), so the named `beads` driver can never be configured. The flywheel's `git merge origin/main` conflicts on `.beads/issues.jsonl` on essentially every divergent integration and must be resolved by hand. The tracked single-JSON `.pan` state files (.pan/specs 233, .pan/continues 147, .pan/records 42) have no merge driver at all.
narratives.Proposal: Fix per file-class: (1) `.beads/issues.jsonl` -> built-in `merge=union` (zero config; concatenates JSONL lines, bd reconciles duplicate ids on load; verified config-free). (2) `.pan/{specs,continues,records}` single-JSON state -> `merge=ours` + wired `merge.ours.driver true` (the documented `true`-command recipe; keeps the current branch's whole file, never corrupts JSON; safe because the on-main pan-dir auto-commit owns these and re-stamps from sources of truth). (3) Add a `pan doctor` guard + unit test that fails on any inert merge attribute (non-built-in `merge=<driver>` with no configured driver) — the root-cause fix that makes PAN-1841's silent inertness loud. (4) Backfill existing clones via an idempotent `ensurePanMergeDrivers` helper wired into workspace/worktree creation AND unconditionally per primary repo in `pan sync`.
narratives.NonGoals:
- State-only main moves resetting feature-branch readyForMerge (PAN-1650 / PAN-1758 / PAN-1765) — the separate other half of 'safe .pan state'.
- The auto-commit/rebase race (PAN-1770) and .pan/specs mutated on feature branches (PAN-1728).
- Refactoring the four scattered `git config beads.role contributor` call sites into one helper, beyond adding the merge-driver line.
- A timestamp-aware or semantic 3-way JSON merge driver for .pan state (the `ours` whole-file strategy is deliberate).
- Healing `beads.role` config on existing repos (only merge-driver config is in scope).
- Any change to `bd` itself or a blanket `*.json merge=union` (would corrupt single-JSON files).

autoDecisions.summary: Use built-in `union` for `.beads/issues.jsonl`, not a real `merge.beads.driver`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified `bd` has no merge-driver subcommand (`bd merge` is unknown; only merge-slot/duplicates exist), so the `beads` driver can never be configured. union is the only viable conflict-free strategy and needs zero config.

autoDecisions.summary: Use `merge=ours` + `git config merge.ours.driver true`, not a bespoke timestamp-aware script driver.
autoDecisions.rationale: A script driver must be present on PATH in every worktree — fragile and the exact failure mode of PAN-1841. `ours` is the documented config-free recipe, never corrupts JSON, and is safe because the auto-commit re-derives state on next flush (a stale-but-valid side self-heals).
A blanket `*.json merge=union` would concatenate hunks of the single-JSON .pan state files into invalid JSON (operator's explicit correction in the issue comments).

autoDecisions.summary: Extend `merge=ours` to `.pan/records/*.json` in addition to the specs/continues named in the issue.
autoDecisions.rationale: Records (42 tracked) are the same single-JSON, per-issue, auto-committed class and conflict identically; covering them is the complete fix.

autoDecisions.summary: `pan doctor` guard is detect-only (reports the inert attribute + heal command), with no `--fix`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps doctor read-only; the heal path is `pan sync` (bead sync-backfill). Avoids scope creep into a doctor auto-repair surface.

items.title: Replace inert `merge=beads` with built-in `merge=union` for `.beads/issues.jsonl`

narrative.Action: In `.gitattributes`, replace the line `.beads/issues.jsonl merge=beads` (and its `# Use bd merge for beads JSONL files` comment) with `.beads/issues.jsonl merge=union`, plus a comment explaining that `union` is a git built-in needing no config, that it concatenates both sides' JSONL lines, and that `bd` reconciles duplicate ids on load. This is the standalone fix for the observed conflict storm and needs no git config.

items.title: `.gitattributes` declares `.beads/issues.jsonl merge=union` and contains no `merge=beads` line
A 3-way merge of two divergent appends to `.beads/issues.jsonl` through the configured attribute produces a union of both sides' lines and emits no `<<<<<<<` conflict markers
After a union merge that introduces a duplicate issue id, `bd list` still loads and parses the file (bd reconciles duplicates on load; no JSON parse error)
Declare `merge=ours` for `.pan` single-JSON state files in `.gitattributes`

narrative.Action: In `.gitattributes`, add `merge=ours` lines for the per-issue single-JSON state families: `.pan/specs/*.vbrief.json`, `.pan/continues/*.vbrief.json`, and `.pan/records/*.json`, with a comment explaining that these are single JSON objects (union would corrupt them) and that `ours` keeps the current branch's whole file and depends on `merge.ours.driver true` being wired by `ensurePanMergeDrivers` (bead driver-config-helper). Depends on the driver config landing so the attribute is not inert (hazard H1).

items.title: `.gitattributes` declares `merge=ours` for `.pan/specs/*.vbrief.json`, `.pan/continues/*.vbrief.json`, and `.pan/records/*.json`

With `merge.ours.driver true` configured, a 3-way merge of two divergent edits to a `.pan/records/*.json` file preserves the current branch's file verbatim and the result parses as valid JSON (no conflict markers)
The same merge through the default driver (no `merge=ours`) instead produces conflict markers — confirming the attribute is what prevents corruption
Add `ensurePanMergeDrivers`/`findInertMergeAttributes` helper and wire config at workspace/worktree creation

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/git-merge-drivers.ts` exporting two functions: `ensurePanMergeDrivers(repoRoot)` which idempotently runs `git config merge.ours.driver true` (best-effort, non-throwing, matching the existing `git config beads.role contributor` sites), and `findInertMergeAttributes(repoRoot)` which parses `.gitattributes` and returns `{pattern, driver}` for every `merge=<driver>` whose `<driver>` is neither a git built-in (`union`/`text`/`binary`) nor backed by a configured `merge.<driver>.driver`. Wire `ensurePanMergeDrivers` into the three worktree/workspace creation sites: `createWorktree` in `src/lib/worktree.ts` (~line 103, Effect `gitRun`), `src/lib/workspace-manager.ts` (~line 402), and `src/cli/commands/workspace.ts` (~line 142) — each immediately after the existing `git config beads.role contributor` call. This helper is the foundation consumed by the doctor guard and the sync backfill.

items.title: `ensurePanMergeDrivers(repoRoot)` sets `git config merge.ours.driver` to `true` in the target repo and is idempotent (a second run leaves a single `true` value and resolves without error)

`findInertMergeAttributes` returns a `{pattern, driver}` entry for a `.gitattributes` declaring `merge=beads` with no configured driver, and returns an empty array when only built-ins (`union`) or configured drivers (`ours` + `merge.ours.driver`) are present
A freshly created worktree reports `git config --get merge.ours.driver` = `true`, proving all three creation sites invoke `ensurePanMergeDrivers`
`ensurePanMergeDrivers` against a non-git directory resolves without throwing (best-effort, non-fatal — matches existing `git config` call-site behavior)
Add `pan doctor` guard + test that fails on inert merge attributes

narrative.Action: Add a `checkInertMergeDrivers()` check to `src/cli/commands/doctor.ts` (following the existing standalone `checkX(): CheckResult[]` pattern, `CheckResult` interface at doctor.ts:161) that calls `findInertMergeAttributes` and returns status `error` (naming each offending pattern + driver and the heal command `pan sync`) when any inert attribute is found, `ok` otherwise. Register it in the doctor run. Add a unit test covering the error case (inert `merge=beads`, no config) and the ok cases (built-in `union`; `merge=ours` with `merge.ours.driver true` configured). Built-in allowlist is exactly `{union, text, binary}` (NFR-1).

items.title: `pan doctor` returns a `CheckResult` with status `error` when `.gitattributes` declares a non-built-in `merge=<driver>` with no configured `merge.<driver>.driver`, and the message names the offending pattern, the driver, and the `pan sync` heal command
The check returns status `ok` when every `merge=` attribute is a built-in (`union`/`text`/`binary`) or has its `merge.<driver>.driver` configured
A unit test asserts both the error case (inert `merge=beads` fixture) and the ok cases (built-in `union`; `merge=ours` + configured driver)
Backfill `merge.ours.driver` onto existing primary repos via `pan sync`

narrative.Action: In `src/cli/commands/sync.ts`, call `ensurePanMergeDrivers(config.path)` for every configured project in the sync loop, UNCONDITIONALLY — outside the beads-DB-missing `catch` block where the existing `git config beads.role contributor` line (line 329) lives, since that catch only fires when the DB is missing and so never re-runs on initialized repos. This makes one `pan sync` heal every already-cloned primary repo (the flywheel's primary main worktree is where the `git merge origin/main` storm occurs, hazard H5).

items.title: `pan sync` invokes `ensurePanMergeDrivers(config.path)` once for every configured project, outside the beads-DB-missing catch block (runs regardless of beads-DB state)
After `pan sync` runs against a project whose primary repo lacked the driver, `git config --get merge.ours.driver` returns `true` in that primary repo
A test asserts the sync loop calls `ensurePanMergeDrivers` once per project independent of whether the beads DB needed initialization
